object: eta_b = sum_{y in mu_n} psi(b y); B = max_b |eta_b|; mu_n = n-th roots of unity, n=2^mu
setting: designed-q: q=p^2 (extension field), exact GF(p^2), n | q-1

LAW1_bad_family.statement: n | (p-1)  <=>  mu_n subset F_p (prime subfield)  =>  B = n EXACTLY (trivial max)
LAW1_bad_family.mechanism: mu_n in F_p; the eigenline alpha*F_p^* (alpha trace-0) gives a coset b*mu_n with Tr=0 identically => eta_b = n
LAW1_bad_family.verified: B==n to 1e-6 for all tested (n,p) with n|p-1; trace-0 coset always exists
LAW1_bad_family.designed_q_meaning: choosing p = 1 mod n MAXIMIZES the Gauss period; these q are the WORST (anti-Ramanujan, B=sqrt(q) at gamma=1, =n always)

LAW2_good_family.statement: n | (p+1)  (mu_n in norm-1 torus, p inert-ish)  =>  SMALLEST B
LAW2_good_family.constant_C: C = B/sqrt(n log(q/n)) in [0.96, 1.4] across n=8,16,32 and wide q
LAW2_good_family.prize_regime_gamma_0.19: C in [0.6,0.9]; B <= sqrt(n log(q/n)) cleanly

UNIVERSAL_SCALING.law: B ~ c * sqrt(n * log(q/n)),  c in [1,1.8]  for ALL non-degenerate classes (B_inert, C_generic)
UNIVERSAL_SCALING.consequence: B/sqrt(n) ~ sqrt(log(q/n)) GROWS; NO designed q=p^2 family reaches clean O(sqrt n) / Ramanujan 2 sqrt(n)
UNIVERSAL_SCALING.reaches_sqrt_n: PARTIAL: reaches sqrt(n*log(q/n)) (matches measured/conjectured), NOT 2 sqrt(n)

semiprimitive_correction.finding:
naive (-1 in

mod n) does NOT predict B~sqrt(q) for thin n; the sqrt(q) blow-up needs -1 in

mod k (k=INDEX=(q-1)/n), which needs k SMALL i.e. n FAT (gamma->1)
semiprimitive_correction.thin_n_prize: for n=q^{0.19}, k~q^{0.81} huge, index-semiprimitive essentially never holds => no semiprimitive sqrt(q) catastrophe; generic sqrt(n log) behavior
